claimText: China controls more than 85% of the global Rare Earth Elements (REE) supply chain and holds nearly half of the world's reserves.
evidenceExcerpt: China controls more than 85% of the global REE supply chain and holds 49% of world reserves.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geopolitics and global distribution of critical minerals.
distractorHooks: USA or Australia controls the majority of the REE supply chain
China holds less than 20% of global REE reserves

claimText: India accounts for 8% of the global Rare Earth Elements (REE) reserves.
evidenceExcerpt: India holds 8% of global REE reserves.
examUse: Statement-based question assessing knowledge of India's strategic mineral capacity.
distractorHooks: India has negligible or no REE reserves
India is the second-largest holder of REE reserves globally

claimText: Rare Earth Elements (REE) reserves in India are primarily extracted from monazite sands.
evidenceExcerpt: India's REE reserves are primarily found in monazite sands.
examUse: Fact-based question on the geological sources of critical minerals in India.
distractorHooks: REEs in India are primarily found in bauxite deposits
REEs are extracted mainly from coal ash in India

claimText: The majority of India's rare earth deposits are located in coastal states such as Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha.
evidenceExcerpt: Coastal states like Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha have the most rare earth deposits in India.
examUse: Geography question on the regional distribution of mineral resources in India.
distractorHooks: Central Indian states like Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh hold the most REE deposits
Himalayan states are the primary source of REE in India
